questions.question: What is the largest continent by area?
questions.explanation: Asia covers about 44.6 million km².

questions.question: What is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile stretches approximately 6,650 km.

questions.question: What is the capital of Japan?
questions.explanation: Tokyo has been Japan's capital since 1868.

questions.question: What is the smallest country in the world by area?
questions.explanation: Vatican City covers just 0.44 km².

questions.question: What strait separates Europe from Africa?
questions.explanation: The Strait of Gibraltar is only 14.3 km wide at its narrowest.

questions.question: Mount Kilimanjaro is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Kilimanjaro is in northeastern Tanzania.

questions.question: What is the deepest point in the ocean?
questions.explanation: Challenger Deep reaches about 10,935 meters.

questions.question: What country has the most time zones?
questions.explanation: France has 12 time zones due to overseas territories.

questions.question: What is the driest inhabited continent?
questions.explanation: Australia receives an average of only 470mm of rain per year.

questions.question: What ocean is the largest?
questions.explanation: The Pacific covers about 165.25 million km².

questions.question: What is the capital of France?
questions.explanation: Paris has been the capital since the 10th century.

questions.question: What is the largest desert in the world?
questions.explanation: The Sahara covers about 9.2 million km² in North Africa.

questions.question: What river runs through London?
questions.explanation: The Thames is 346 km long.

questions.question: What is the tallest mountain in the world?
questions.explanation: Everest stands at 8,849 meters.

questions.question: What country has the largest population?
questions.explanation: India surpassed China as the most populous country in 2023.

questions.question: On which continent is Brazil located?
questions.explanation: Brazil is the largest country in South America.

questions.question: What is the capital of Australia?
questions.explanation: Canberra was chosen as a compromise between Sydney and Melbourne.

questions.question: What sea lies between Europe and Africa?
questions.explanation: The Mediterranean connects to the Atlantic through the Strait of Gibraltar.

questions.question: What is the largest lake in Africa?
questions.explanation: Lake Victoria is the world's second-largest freshwater lake by area.

questions.question: What country is known as the Land of the Rising Sun?
questions.explanation: Japan's name (Nihon/Nippon) literally means 'origin of the sun.'

questions.question: What is the longest mountain range in the world?
questions.explanation: The Andes stretch about 7,000 km along South America's west coast.

questions.question: What island nation is off the southeast coast of Africa?
questions.explanation: Madagascar is the fourth-largest island in the world.

questions.question: What is the capital of Canada?
questions.explanation: Ottawa became the capital in 1857.

questions.question: What river is the longest in Europe?
questions.explanation: The Volga flows 3,530 km through Russia.

questions.question: What country is both in Europe and Asia?
questions.explanation: Istanbul straddles the Bosporus, connecting Europe and Asia.

questions.question: What is the only country that borders both the Atlantic and Indian Oceans?
questions.explanation: South Africa's coast stretches from the Atlantic to the Indian Ocean.

questions.question: What is the highest waterfall in the world?
questions.explanation: Angel Falls in Venezuela drops 979 meters.

questions.question: What is the Ring of Fire?
questions.correctAnswer: A zone of volcanic and seismic activity around the Pacific
questions.explanation: About 75% of the world's volcanoes are in the Ring of Fire.

questions.question: What is the world's largest archipelago?
questions.explanation: Indonesia comprises over 17,000 islands.

questions.question: What is the capital of Mongolia?
questions.explanation: Ulaanbaatar is home to nearly half of Mongolia's population.

questions.question: What continent is Egypt in?
questions.explanation: Egypt is in the northeast corner of Africa.

questions.question: What is the capital of Germany?
questions.explanation: Berlin has been the capital of reunified Germany since 1990.

questions.question: What is the smallest ocean?
questions.explanation: The Arctic Ocean covers about 14.06 million km².

questions.question: What landlocked country is between France and Spain?
questions.explanation: Andorra is a tiny principality in the Pyrenees mountains.

questions.question: What African country was formerly known as Abyssinia?
questions.explanation: Ethiopia is one of the oldest nations in the world.

questions.question: What is the largest country in South America?
questions.explanation: Brazil covers 8.5 million km², nearly half of South America.

questions.question: What is the Mariana Trench?
questions.correctAnswer: The deepest part of the world's oceans
questions.explanation: The Mariana Trench reaches nearly 11 km deep in the Pacific.

questions.question: What country has the most official languages?
questions.explanation: South Africa has 11 official languages.

questions.question: What is the capital of Italy?
questions.explanation: Rome has been Italy's capital since 1871.

questions.question: Which country is an island continent?
questions.explanation: Australia is both the smallest continent and the largest island.

questions.question: What is the most visited country in the world?
questions.explanation: France receives about 90 million international tourists per year.

questions.question: What two countries share the longest border?
questions.explanation: The US-Canada border is about 8,891 km long.

questions.question: What is the largest landlocked country?
questions.explanation: Kazakhstan covers 2.7 million km² in Central Asia.

questions.question: What is the name of the supercontinent that existed 200 million years ago?
questions.explanation: Pangaea began breaking apart about 200 million years ago.

questions.question: What is the capital of Spain?
questions.explanation: Madrid is located in the center of the Iberian Peninsula.

questions.question: What is the largest island in the world?
questions.explanation: Greenland covers about 2.17 million km².

questions.question: What country is known as the Pearl of the Orient?
questions.explanation: The Philippines earned this nickname for its natural beauty.

questions.question: What is the world's saltiest body of water?
questions.explanation: Don Juan Pond in Antarctica has a salinity of over 40%.

questions.question: What river flows through Egypt?
questions.explanation: The Nile has been the lifeblood of Egyptian civilization for millennia.

questions.question: What country has the most natural lakes?
questions.explanation: Canada has over 2 million lakes, more than any other country.
